title: asteraidae
intro: The Asterales is a taxonomical rank, specifically a name given to a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics. It is a division within the larger group of plants and is often used to describe a group of related families. The Asterales order includes a wide range of plants such as daisies, asters, and sunflowers, among others. Examples of orders within the Asterales include Asparagales, Malpighiales, and Poales.

orders.order_desc: Campanulales is an order of flowering plants that belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, subclass Asteridae, clade Angiosperms, and kingdom Plantae.
Polemoniales belongs to the taxonomical classification of plants as follows: an order within the class Magnoliopsida.
Dipsacales is an order of flowering plants that includes the honeysuckle family, as well as others such as the honeysuckle-like woodwaxen and the foxglove and larkspur.
